Bibliography
Home

Bibliography

References and sources that inspired our exploration of French cuisine

Books

Mastering the Art of French Cooking

Author: Julia Child, Simone Beck, Louisette Bertholle
Year: 1961
Publisher: Alfred A. Knopf

The definitive guide to French cooking for American audiences, featuring classic techniques and recipes.

Larousse Gastronomique

Author: Prosper Montagné
Year: 1938
Publisher: Larousse

The world's most famous culinary encyclopedia, covering French cuisine, techniques, and ingredients.

Le Guide Culinaire

Author: Auguste Escoffier
Year: 1903
Publisher: Flammarion

The foundational text of modern French cuisine, codifying the five mother sauces and classic techniques.

French Cooking Classics

Author: Ginette Mathiot
Year: 1932
Publisher: Albin Michel

A comprehensive collection of traditional French home cooking recipes loved by generations.

The World Atlas of Wine

Author: Hugh Johnson, Jancis Robinson
Year: 1971
Publisher: Mitchell Beazley

An authoritative guide to wine regions of the world, with extensive coverage of French vineyards.

Websites

Atelier Tatin

Visit

https://www.ateliertatin.com

Official site of the Tarte Tatin sisters' legacy, featuring the history of this iconic upside-down tart.

Fromages de France

Visit

https://www.fromagesdefrance.com

The official portal for French cheese, profiling hundreds of AOP and traditional cheeses.

Bordeaux Wine Official

Visit

https://www.bordeaux.com

The official website of Bordeaux wines, offering guides to appellations, tastings, and châteaux.

Maison de Campagne

Visit

https://www.maisondecampagne.fr

A digital magazine dedicated to French regional cuisine, seasonal ingredients, and rural gastronomy.

Media

Chef's Table: France

Netflix Documentary Series•2016

An intimate look into the lives and kitchens of Michelin-starred French chefs.

Ratatouille

Animated Film•2007

Pixar's love letter to French cuisine, celebrating the idea that anyone can cook.

A Taste of Provence

BBC Documentary•2019

A journey through the sun-drenched landscapes and flavors of Provence.

“The fine arts are five in number: painting, sculpture, poetry, music, and architecture, the principal branch of the latter being pastry.”

— Marie-Antoine Carême

“La gastronomie est l'art d'utiliser la nourriture pour créer le bonheur.”
